finding: Previous generated-art artifacts made the next session ambiguous about whether Otis was live, prototype, or reset.
action: Keep the engine, but clear old generated runs, public Otis art, and approved character manifest entries before the next production attempt.

finding: Studio orientation checks whether the user needs a guided creative brief.
action: Continue gathering creative intent before creating production packets.

finding: Studio orientation checks whether the user needs a guided creative brief.
action: Continue gathering creative intent before creating production packets.

finding: Studio orientation checks whether the user needs a guided creative brief.
action: Continue gathering creative intent before creating production packets.

finding: Budgeted Gemini API initial concepts completed with warning receipts for missing alpha and JPEG mime, so concept review is possible but production promotion remains blocked.
action: Do not promote concept outputs; use the repair plan and stricter production-pack prompting after Armaan selects the Otis identity.

finding: The neutral-background initial concept prompt fixed the v1 green matte contamination, but provider receipts still have missing-alpha and JPEG warnings, so final app promotion stays blocked.
action: Treat v2 as the identity-choice board and rely on the production-pack repair path for alpha-clean sprites after selection.

finding: Budgeted initial concept requests now satisfy the initial direction gate, create an API directive with character canon notes, and use neutral concept backgrounds while production packs retain the #00ff00 matte requirement.
action: Resume concept review with final app promotion still gated behind approved for app and strict production-pack QA.

finding: Armaan rejected v2 as a style-failure board: the concepts read as muted storybook/editorial illustration rather than rich, sharp, premium Tower web-game character art.
action: Mark v2 superseded, do not proceed with Lane 05, add a reusable initial-concept style-quality contract, and regenerate five fresh prompt-only Otis concepts from scratch under the configured budget cap.

finding: Initial character concept prompts did not carry a reusable style-quality contract strong enough to prevent muted storybook/editorial output.
action: Added a reusable initial-concept style-quality contract for premium web-game dialogue sprite quality, positive and negative style anchors, natural human imperfections, lower prompt repetition, and exact approved-for-app promotion gating tests.

finding: The reusable style contract produced five richer, sharper Otis concepts with stronger burgundy/brass/deep navy palette and material detail, but the board remains concept-only with JPEG/no-alpha receipt warnings plus some lane-specific prop text or crop risks.
action: Use v3 only for identity-direction selection; after Armaan chooses a direction, regenerate a production pack with strict full-body staging, no readable prop text, and matte/alpha requirements.

finding: V3 was useful progress but not final because it produced a quality ramp: lanes varied from lower detail to higher detail instead of sharing one premium rendering floor.
action: Mark v3 useful-but-not-final, add a permanent shared lane quality floor, separate identity variation from rendering quality, and regenerate five fresh prompt-only Otis concepts with no old references.

finding: Initial concept lane mandates could be interpreted as varying rendering quality/detail rather than only identity direction.
action: Every initial concept lane now receives the same minimum quality/detail floor, including Lane 05-level material detail, fabric seams, buttons, hair/beard rendering, brass highlights, confident contrast, dimensional lighting, and high-resolution premium game-sprite finish. Lane-specific text is now a separate unique identity mandate.

finding: V4 generated five prompt-only Otis concepts with a more consistent premium detail floor across lanes. The board is still concept-only because the receipts have JPEG/no-alpha warnings and several lanes would need production-pack cleanup for crop, prop scribbles, or staging.
action: Use v4 only for identity-direction selection. After Armaan chooses a direction, create a production pack with strict full-body staging, no readable prop text, and matte/alpha requirements.

finding: Armaan selected Lane 05 as the Otis identity direction; the selected concept still contains concept-only desk/background and prop-scribble risks.
action: Lock Lane 05 as identity DNA, keep production promotion blocked, and make the next production packet preserve the selected face/silhouette/wardrobe while cleaning background, prop text, full-body staging, and matte/alpha readiness.

finding: The selected Gemini concept had PNG filenames but JPEG bytes, which would make future reference-image MIME handling ambiguous.
action: Convert the locked identity copies to true PNG files before using them as approved identity references for the production run.

finding: Studio orientation checks whether the user needs a guided creative brief.
action: Continue gathering creative intent before creating production packets.

finding: Studio orientation checks whether the user needs a guided creative brief.
action: Continue gathering creative intent before creating production packets.

finding: Gemini API generated all 24 Otis production-pack sources at 4K-class size, but both attempts shaded the #00ff00 background with gradients, contact shadows, or green-screen lighting. Strict repair-plan correctly rejected alpha extraction for every slot because no latest source met the safe flat matte threshold.
action: Do not promote or call the board upload-ready. Keep the v001/v002 generations as blocked evidence, preserve the budget guard, and require a stronger provider strategy before any alpha extraction: either true transparent PNG support, a provider mode that honors pixel-flat matte, or a separately approved non-Gemini/manual matte-prep path.

finding: The Otis production-pack failure proved the engine was discovering provider alpha/matte failures after full-pack spend.
action: Added the art:produce orchestrator, formal run state machine, provider capability profiles, generation budget ledger, production canary/full-plan firewall, canary verification gate, slot-only retry filters, repair-auto, shader routing, status/docs hardening, and focused/stress tests so full-pack paid generation cannot run until a representative canary proves the provider contract.
